global_series_rules.2020: Back-cast from the public 2024 level using the public 12% per year historical growth note.
global_series_rules.2025: Linear interpolation between 2024 = 415 TWh and 2030 = 945 TWh.
global_series_rules.2035: Direct case endpoint from Energy and AI.
global_series_rules.post_2035: Hold the 2035 case level constant through 2100.

query_titles: total final energy by aggregate sector
primary energy consumption by region (direct equivalent)
primary energy consumption by region (avg fossil efficiency)
validation_query_titles: total final energy by aggregate sector
primary energy consumption by region (direct equivalent)

academic: Masanet et al. (2020), Recalibrating global data center energy-use estimates

source_mapping.SSP1: Energy and AI High Efficiency case endpoint
source_mapping.SSP2: Energy and AI Base Case endpoint
source_mapping.SSP3: Energy and AI Headwinds case endpoint
source_mapping.SSP4: Energy and AI Headwinds case endpoint
source_mapping.SSP5: Energy and AI Lift-Off case endpoint

method_notes.historic: The explicit datacenter sector remains zero in 1975-2010 and uses only a tiny 2015 seed to preserve GCAM calibration stability.
method_notes.global_series: The datacenter series now starts from a single IEA edition, Energy and AI 2025. It uses the public 2024 level, a 2020 back-cast from the public 12% annual growth note, a 2025 linear bridge, the public 2030 Base Case, and the public 2035 case endpoints.
method_notes.regionalization: Regionalization now begins from public 2024 IEA shares: USA 45%, China 25%, Europe 15%, with the residual treated as rest of world. For 2030, the public USA +240 TWh increment is applied explicitly, and the remaining 2030 total is split across non-USA regions in proportion to the 2024 public shares. Within each anchored group, GCAM's 2015 commercial electricity calibration is used only as an intra-group split.
method_notes.post_2035: No speculative post-2035 growth is imposed. The 2035 case level and the 2035 regional share structure are held constant through 2100.
method_notes.gcam_mapping: The datacenter module is represented as a new electricity-only supplysector and a matching energy-final-demand sector named comm datacenter sector. Final energy therefore appears directly in GCAM final-energy queries, while primary energy remains an endogenous system response.

provenance_audit_2026_03_22.status: Anchored to one IEA edition, Energy and AI 2025, with explicit bridge, regionalization, and flat post-2035 extension formulas.
public_iea_values_verified: 2024 regional shares = United States 45%, China 25%, Europe 15%
computed_from_source_or_repo_rules: 2030 non-USA regional allocation from 2024 public shares after applying the public USA increment
within-group GCAM regional splitting by 2015 commercial electricity
direct_iea_claims_that_should_not_be_mad: That 2020 or 2025 are direct IEA datacenter levels
That all 2030 regional datacenter levels are direct IEA outputs
That 2040-2100 datacenter growth is published by IEA

reference_shortlist: Masanet, E., Shehabi, A., Lei, N., Smith, S. and Koomey, J. 2020. Recalibrating global data center energy-use estimates. Science 367(6481): 984-986. https://doi.org/10.1126/science.aba3758
Shehabi, A. et al. 2024. 2024 United States Data Center Energy Usage Report. Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory. https://doi.org/10.71468/P1WC7Q
